channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: In Vermont, small town meetings grapple with debate on big issues
item.description: Typically concerned with local issues, residents at town meetings in Vermont and elsewhere increasingly use the forum to debate polarizing national and international events.
item.content:encoded:
Typically concerned with local issues, residents at town meetings in Vermont and elsewhere increasingly use the forum to debate polarizing national and international events.

(Image credit: JOSEPH PREZIOSO)
item.dc:creator: Tovia Smith

item.title: Supreme Court blocks redrawing of New York congressional map, dealing a win for GOP
item.description: At issue is the mid-term redrawing of New York's 11th Congressional District, including Staten Island and a small part of Brooklyn.
item.content:encoded:
At issue is the mid-term redrawing of New York's 11th Congressional District, including Staten Island and a small part of Brooklyn.

(Image credit: Win McNamee)
item.dc:creator: Zoe Sobel

item.title: Supreme Court wrestles with gun rights, marijuana, and the right to own a gun
item.description: The argument took place in light of the court's 2022 Bruen decision, which held that for a gun law to be constitutional, it must be analogous to a similar law at the nation's founding in the late 1700s.
item.content:encoded:
The argument took place in light of the court's 2022 Bruen decision, which held that for a gun law to be constitutional, it must be analogous to a similar law at the nation's founding in the late 1700s.

(Image credit: Alex Wroblewski)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: Videos of Bill and Hillary Clinton's depositions in Epstein probe are released
item.description: Republicans on the House Oversight Committee have released videos of Bill and Hillary Clinton's depositions in the committee's ongoing investigation into the Epstein files.
item.content:encoded: Republicans on the House Oversight Committee have released videos of Bill and Hillary Clinton's depositions in the committee's ongoing investigation into the Epstein files.
item.dc:creator: Claudia Grisales

item.title: What Americans make of the U.S. attack on Iran so far
item.description: As missiles continue to fly across the Middle East, what do Americans make of the U.S. attack on Iran so far? Residents of Harrisburg, Pa., weigh in.
item.content:encoded: As missiles continue to fly across the Middle East, what do Americans make of the U.S. attack on Iran so far? Residents of Harrisburg, Pa., weigh in.
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt

item.title: This woman is providing second-hand clothes that teens don't just need, but want
item.description: Donated clothes for low-income kids aren't always that cool. So a volunteer at a school district in Sonoma County curates, launders and presents clothing donations that teens actually want to wear.
item.content:encoded: Donated clothes for low-income kids aren't always that cool. So a volunteer at a school district in Sonoma County curates, launders and presents clothing donations that teens actually want to wear.
item.dc:creator: Shandra Back

item.title: Some Middle East flights resume, but thousands of travelers are still stranded by war
item.description: Limited flights out of the Middle East resumed on Monday. But hundreds of thousands of travelers are still stranded in the region after attacks on Iran by the U.S. and Israel.
item.content:encoded:
Limited flights out of the Middle East resumed on Monday. But hundreds of thousands of travelers are still stranded in the region after attacks on Iran by the U.S. and Israel.

(Image credit: Fadel Senna)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: Trump defends Iran strikes, offers objectives for military operation
item.description: His remarks are the first public ones to reporters since the U.S.-Israeli military operation against Iran began Saturday despite weeks of talks designed to stave off a conflict.
item.content:encoded:
His remarks are the first public ones to reporters since the U.S.-Israeli military operation against Iran began Saturday despite weeks of talks designed to stave off a conflict.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk

item.title: Supreme Court ponders law making it a crime for marijuana users to own guns
item.description: The law, the same one used to prosecute Joe Biden's son for illegal gun possession, has united an array of strange bedfellows, from conservative gun rights groups to liberal civil liberties groups.
item.content:encoded:
The law, the same one used to prosecute Joe Biden's son for illegal gun possession, has united an array of strange bedfellows, from conservative gun rights groups to liberal civil liberties groups.

(Image credit: Heather Diehl)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: Congress gears up for vote on Trump's war powers in Iran — after the battle began
item.description: Democrats and a few Republicans are calling for curbing President Trump's unilateral use of military power in Iran, despite previous such efforts failing to advance.
item.content:encoded:
Democrats and a few Republicans are calling for curbing President Trump's unilateral use of military power in Iran, despite previous such efforts failing to advance.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett
